title: CUBE MDX escaped-member references and measure-grain boundary
originSignal: A CUBE formula contains escaped member text and returns an unexpected value at a different aggregation grain.
riskPattern: Escaped MDX parsing can obscure member references while numerator and denominator filters operate at incompatible grains.
regressionChecks: Extract escaped MDX member and measure references from synthetic CUBE formulas.
Trace helper-cell and direct references separately.
Record the intended measure grain and distinguish it from formula parsing evidence.
expectedEvidence: MDX reference extraction records escaped members and parsing diagnostics.
CUBE dependency report identifies formula, measure, and helper-cell relationships.
Synthetic notes state the grain question that needs live model evaluation.
boundaries: Formula-text inspection cannot evaluate CUBEVALUE or validate Data Model aggregation behavior.
Only a sanitized live workbook in the target Excel host can prove member resolution and measure grain.
nextLiveWorkbookRequirement: Evaluate a sanitized workbook containing escaped members, helper cells, and contrasting grains in Excel desktop.
